_note: goodFor for batch-5 (10 programs, 2026-07-07). Same conventions as good-for.json/good-for-3.json/good-for-4.json. Apply with prisma/apply-good-for.ts AFTER prisma/import-researched.ts researched-programs-5.json has created these rows.

programs.goodFor: For first-year HUC-JIR rabbinical, cantorial and Jewish-education graduate students (enrollment is automatic upon admission, not separately applied for) wanting a required, immersive year that uses Israel itself as the classroom.

For JTS rabbinical students (required as part of the degree, not separately applied for) wanting a year of advanced Jewish text study at the Schechter Institute alongside deep engagement with Israeli society and history.
For Ziegler rabbinical students (required, not separately applied for) wanting an intensive 10-12 week Israel immersion as part of their Conservative ordination track.
For Reconstructionist Rabbinical College students (required, not separately applied for) wanting a summer term of Israel immersion, with the option to extend to a full year via RRC scholarships.

For members of the secular, Labor-Zionist Hashomer Hatzair youth movement (active in a dozen-plus countries) wanting a 10-month communal-living gap year in Israel before returning home to lead the movement.

For Revisionist-Zionist Betar movement members wanting an 8-month gap year mixing leadership training alongside 100+ peers from other youth movements worldwide, a taste of IDF service, and touring in Israel and Poland.
For religious-Zionist gap-year students specifically from Australia, New Zealand, South Africa or South America wanting Torah study paired with self-empowerment and personal-growth programming.

For 18-22-year-old European, Israeli and American Jews of any observance level wanting a 10-month program blending Israel immersion, University of Oslo coursework, and travel to Jewish communities worldwide.

For CAMERA Fellows and campus Israel-advocacy activists (US, Canada, Uruguay) wanting an intensive 2-week Israel mission of expert speakers and touring to sharpen their advocacy skills before returning to campus.

For trained contemporary dancers (men 18-28, women 18-25) from Israel and worldwide wanting professional-level training at a kibbutz-based dance company recognized by Israel's Ministry of Culture & Sports.
